step3_backend_registration.command: python -c 'import torch; import lstm_sequence_encoder_optimized; assert "lstm_sequence_encoder_opt" in str(torch._dynamo.list_backends())'

test_results.test_get_model_and_input: PASS — input shape (32,128,256) BF16 on CUDA, model BF16

pass_pretranspose_classifier.reason: Dynamo produces 0 traceable FX graphs for nn.LSTM models. torch._VF.lstm (the cuDNN entry point) is not traced by TorchDynamo — it creates an immediate graph break. As a result, the backend function lstm_sequence_encoder_opt() is never called, and no FX passes execute.
pass_pretranspose_classifier.impact: None — the addmm was 0.7% of baseline time. The pass was included for completeness only.
pass_pretranspose_classifier.recommendation: For LSTM models, FX graph passes targeting the classifier head should be implemented via a custom nn.Module.forward() override instead, or the model should be restructured so the classifier is in a separately-compilable submodule.

OPT1_bf16.evidence: test_get_model_and_input: x.dtype == torch.bfloat16, model params dtype == torch.bfloat16. Compiled and eager outputs numerically identical (allclose atol=0.05).

OPT2_cudnn_benchmark.evidence: torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True set in get_model_and_input(). cudnn.benchmark caches fastest algorithm on first warmup call.

OPT3_pretranspose.reason: FX backend never invoked by Dynamo for this model architecture.

warnings: RNN module weights not contiguous after BF16 cast — flatten_parameters() called on each forward pass. This is expected and handled by warmup iterations in the profiling pipeline.

Dynamo graph count = 0 for nn.LSTM — model runs through cuDNN in eager mode even when torch.compile is specified. This is a known limitation of TorchDynamo with cuDNN RNN operators in PyTorch 2.11. The primary optimizations (BF16 + cudnn.benchmark) apply at the eager level and do not require Dynamo compilation.
